# Questions in Livestream 230

Live Stream #230: Ten Things About One Thing

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=786" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:06</a> | Why didn't early alphabetic writers base their letters on the Egyptian uniliteral signs with the same sounds? | He says the reason is unknown and notes that other languages also borrowed scripts without preserving the source phonetic system. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=890" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:50</a> | What do you make of the marijuana-use claim at Tel Arad? | He is skeptical and jokes that contamination or later misuse of the site is more plausible than sensational conclusions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=978" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:18</a> | Would modern contamination and ancient use have been identifiable as different test results? | Not necessarily; he says sample sizes and residue concentrations can be too small to distinguish ancient from modern contamination without further testing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=1189" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:49</a> | Why would Pharaoh Neco change Eliakim's name to Jehoiakim and add a Yahweh element? | He suggests Neco likely needed an administrative way to distinguish this vassal from other rulers with El names, not that Neco cared about Yahweh. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=1312" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:52</a> | Did you write today? | He says progress is progress, then urges viewers to contribute creatively because arts and writing can shape culture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=1417" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:37</a> | Does sermon writing count as creative work? | Yes, if the pastor is doing the actual text work and writing rather than recycling canned sermon material. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=1636" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:16</a> | Is your fiction making an impact, and do non-Christians read it? | He says the answer is yes if non-Christians are reading it, because the goal is to reach outside the Christian bubble. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=1892" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:32</a> | How should Genesis 14:13's Hebrew reference to Abram be understood? | He thinks it is a corruption or later shaping of a term related to apiru/habiru, a class designation for sojourners, mercenaries, and outsiders. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=2121" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:21</a> | What is the best understanding of Adam being alone in Genesis 2:18 before the fall? | He says creation was not yet perfected; Adam being alone was not evil, but it was not the completed good God was moving creation toward. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=2405" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:05</a> | Why does Genesis 14:18 mention bread and wine? | He treats it as basic hospitality: bread and wine were common staples to offer important guests, especially an armed visitor like Abram. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=2623" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:43</a> | What are the best and worst reasons you have heard for reading or not reading the Bible? | He says the best reason to read is to know God better; the worst is to trip up others, while confusion can be a reasonable reason someone hesitates to read. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=2729" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:29</a> | If Genesis 14:20 does not name Abram as giving the tithe, how do we know Abram gave it? | He says the context makes Abram the sensible giver, since Melchizedek giving Abram a tenth of Salem would not fit the scene. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=2899" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:19</a> | Does God send visions when he believes they are necessary? | Yes, but he says such visions must be tested by Scripture and are usually personal rather than new revelation for the whole church. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=3161" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:41</a> | How long would Abraham have been with Abimelech for Genesis 20:17-18 to be noticed? | He says at least nine months and probably a few years, since a broad fertility stoppage would need time to become evident. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=3366" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:06</a> | Which Bible passage makes you facepalm because of secondhand embarrassment? | He names Jephthah's speech in Judges, saying it is full of errors and shows how ignorant Jephthah is. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=3478" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:58</a> | In Genesis 20:12, were Abraham and Sarah half-siblings or is another kinship custom involved? | He says they could be half-siblings, but Hebrew kinship terms can also cover more distant relations such as grandparents, aunts, uncles, or cousins. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=3689" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:29</a> | Is there evidence that Epiphany or Christmas was celebrated in the first, second, or third century AD? | He says there is no first-century evidence, some late second-century evidence, and definite evidence by the third century, especially around Epiphany. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=3771" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:51</a> | In Genesis 19:7, is the wickedness sexual relations or mistreating guests? | He says the context points to sexual wrongdoing, because Lot's proposed substitution of his daughters would not answer a mere hospitality issue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=3982" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:22</a> | What separates the clan of Enoch from the patriarch Enoch, and why is Enoch not a rapture archetype? | He says Enoch is the notable final progenitor of the clan, and his being taken is a poor rapture archetype because it is not a judgment-removal text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=4180" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:40</a> | How is the rapture not really biblical, and is it opposite to the common depiction? | He argues the common prooftexts describe judgment first, so the ones "taken" are the wicked being removed, not believers escaping beforehand. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=4868" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:08</a> | In Genesis 19:5, why did the men of Sodom act that way? | He gives no complex explanation beyond saying they were wicked people doing evil things. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=4914" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:54</a> | Does Lot sitting at the gate in Genesis 19:1 imply he was well esteemed? | It implies he held some elder or dispute-settling role, though he says that does not necessarily mean moral esteem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=5241" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:21</a> | How do you reconcile the different genealogies of Jesus in Matthew and Luke? | He follows the common view that one line runs through Joseph and the other through Mary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=5357" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:17</a> | Why is Abraham so insistent that the three visitors stay in Genesis 18? | He treats it as a strong act of hospitality in the setting just before the Sodom episode. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=5621" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:41</a> | Besides Exodus, which biblical book is most important to you, and why? | He names Genesis, Isaiah, and Jeremiah as his main candidates for ritual, messianic, and sacrificial-system reasons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=5753" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:53</a> | Why were slaves circumcised in Genesis 17:13? | He says male servants were brought under the covenant household, so circumcision marked inclusion in that covenant order. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=6076" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:16</a> | Can we assume Adam was never immortal, since Genesis 3:22 says he had not yet eaten from the tree of life? | He says the passage is debated, but it at least leaves room for Adam not yet possessing immortality. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=6305" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:05</a> | Where does the idea of not seeing God and living come from, if Genesis 16 comes earlier chronologically? | He answers by treating Hagar's experience as a mediated theophany rather than the same kind of direct vision later warnings address. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=6702" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:42</a> | Is it possible that Moses, Aaron, and Miriam were not literal siblings but members of the same Amramite clan? | He says it is possible and warns against being too dogmatic that the sibling terms must mean literal siblings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=6764" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:44</a> | What does Genesis 16:12 mean? | He says Ishmael is being described as stubborn, combative, untamable, and living east of his brothers in the wilderness regions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=6900" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:00</a> | With increasing digital ID requirements, what is the possibility of a mark of the beast? | He warns against newspaper prophecy and says the mark is more likely tied to allegiance to the beast's teaching than simply to a digital ID. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=7517" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:05:17</a> | Why does Sarah blame Abraham in Genesis 16:4-5? | He says Sarah's scheme backfires and she turns the resulting humiliation against Abraham. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=7889" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:29</a> | How do people who read Genesis 6:3 as a lifespan limit interpret Genesis 25:7 and Numbers 33:39? | He says they usually see the long patriarchal ages as a post-flood diminishing curve toward a 120-year maximum, though he prefers the countdown-to-flood reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=8282" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:02</a> | What biblical scene do artists always get wrong that you would like to see done accurately? | He says Ruth, especially because she is often depicted as a young girl when the story presents her as an adult widow. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=8758" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:58</a> | Which Aram is meant in Genesis 22:21? | He says the options include an ordinary person named Aram, ethnography, or retrojection, and he leans toward an ordinary person named Aram. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=9033" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:33</a> | Does the plural form *elohim* in Genesis 1 refer to God and the sons of God together? | He says no and treats it there as a majestic plural rather than a true numerical plural. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=9291" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:51</a> | In Genesis 24:47, did the ring really go into Rebekah's nose? | Yes; he says it is a nose ring. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=10236" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:36</a> | Who is the commander in Joshua 5:13-15? | He treats the figure as a Christophany because the command to remove sandals marks the ground as holy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=10426" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:46</a> | Does Genesis 25:20 show that Abraham was Aramean? | He says the Aramean language in that verse applies to Rebekah's family setting, not to Abraham as an ethnic reclassification. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=10967" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:47</a> | How should we study the book of Joshua? | He says Joshua is didactic, organized from good tribes to bad tribes, and should be read as teaching Israel through examples of obedience and failure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=11192" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:32</a> | How did Israelite identity become distinct from Amorite identity? | He treats the Exodus as the nation-forming event that turned the sons of Jacob and the mixed multitude into Israel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=11649" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:14:09</a> | If Abraham was Amorite, would that make his origins Hamitic through Genesis 10? | He says no, because Genesis 10 is ethnography rather than strict genealogy; Amorites are culturally assigned there, not genealogically derived from Ham. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=11719" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:15:19</a> | What statutes and laws did Abraham keep in Genesis 26:5? | He says the covenant requirements Abraham personally received, especially circumcision, are part of the answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=11890" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:10</a> | How should Matthew 19:23-26 be understood in context? | He reads it as a warning about the spiritual danger of riches, not as a wooden slogan detached from the disciples' panic in context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=12664" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:31:04</a> | What are treasures in heaven? | He says the greatest treasure is relational: face time with Christ, not gold or material wealth. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=12963" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:36:03</a> | Could time be experienced differently after the resurrection? | He allows that it could be somewhat different, but says Scripture still presents causal sequence, so one should not rely on that speculation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=13022" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:02</a> | Does Genesis 27:29 imply Isaac and Rebekah had more children? | He says the language is ambiguous and likely prophetic, referring to peoples and descendants rather than proving extra named sons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=13265" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:41:05</a> | Was Rahab simply a prostitute, or are we getting her role wrong? | He says she functioned more like an innkeeper or madam whose establishment also made sense as a place for clandestine political activity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=13616" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:46:56</a> | Why did Esau care about not being blessed in Genesis 27:34? | He says the blessing was about divine approval, clan leadership, and public respect, unlike the birthright, which was mainly property. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=13913" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:53</a> | Is Rahab's inn why city authorities came to her specifically? | Yes; he says an inn was exactly where suspicious foreign visitors would likely go. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=13934" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:52:14</a> | Would Esau's birthright sale have mattered legally without Isaac's approval? | Yes; he says the sale could still matter because inheritance property could be transferred and settled after Isaac's death. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=14318" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:58:38</a> | What life lessons did David and Kiara learn from their parents and grandparents? | He gives a personal answer, saying he mostly received shallow platitudes rather than deep life lessons from his parents. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=14480" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:01:20</a> | What advice do you have for Christians dealing with existential dread? | He says the cure is understanding human significance before God rather than measuring worth by cosmic size. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=14876" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:07:56</a> | Does mentioning the Westminster Confession reliably indicate your denomination? | He says no; citing it does not function as a denomination reveal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=14945" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:09:05</a> | Why couldn't Isaac simply bless Esau differently? | He says the chief blessing concerned singular clan leadership and could not just be duplicated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=15035" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:10:35</a> | When was Esau's Genesis 27:40 prophecy fulfilled? | He ties it to Moab's later sword-based existence, service under Omri, and revolt under Mesha. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=15157" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:12:37</a> | Why would Rebekah lose both sons in one day in Genesis 27:45? | He says Esau would have needed to kill Jacob and Isaac to become clan chief, and the attempt could also get Esau killed by the servants. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=15250" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:14:10</a> | Is Islam a particularly concerning religion or just one fallacy among many? | He says it is concerning in the West because secularism creates a religious vacuum, though he cautions against weak anti-Islam arguments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=15766" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:22:46</a> | Why does Laban say everything is his in Genesis 31:43? | He says Laban treated his daughters, grandchildren, and flocks as extensions of his own clan property and could not let go. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=16062" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:27:42</a> | What made angels stand out from men in the Bible? | He says angels in similacrum form could look human unless their glory showed; their native form is different and not reproductive flesh. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=16343" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:32:23</a> | Please explain Genesis 32:24-30. | He says Jacob wrestles a divine figure before meeting Esau, receives a wound and a blessing, and is renamed Israel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=16654" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:37:34</a> | Does crushing the serpent's head in Genesis 3:15 refer to Jesus on the cross or to Satan's final destruction? | He says the heel-bruising happens at the cross, while the serpent's decisive defeat is secured by the resurrection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=16980" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:43:00</a> | Does Genesis 33:20 mean Jacob identified Yahweh as El? | He says the patriarchs knew God through El titles before the name Yahweh was introduced through Moses, with later Yahweh references retrojected in the text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=17105" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:45:05</a> | Do you think Randy Alcorn's book *Heaven* is accurate? | He has not read it, so he will not judge it, but says detailed books about heaven usually raise yellow flags for him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=17168" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:46:08</a> | What does "in Israel" mean in Genesis 34:7? | He says the Dinah incident is framed as a grave clan violation that threatened tribal honor and security, not merely private wrongdoing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=17718" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:55:18</a> | Did God force Pharaoh against his will by hardening his heart? | He says no; Pharaoh first hardens his own heart twice, and only then does God harden it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=17796" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:56:36</a> | In Genesis 34:29, what would Jacob's sons have done with the women and children? | He says women and girls would likely become slaves, concubines, or wives, while male posterity would be killed under clan vendetta logic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=17955" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:59:15</a> | Would other Hivite tribes have wanted revenge after the Genesis 34 attack? | He says no; once the vendetta was satisfied, other clans would recognize the rules of clan honor and stay out of it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fsOH2nJfj7o?t=18251" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:04:11</a> | In Genesis 1, is God creating order out of chaos, chaos out of order, or starting from order? | He says none of those exactly: Genesis presents unformed, empty potential, not chaos, and God gives it form, purpose, and goodness. |
